LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC. AND DEBORAH LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS A MATTER OF LAW. THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION BY THE RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION BOARD DID NOT ACTUALLY LITIGATE MS. LOPEZ'S INTENT. MS. LOPEZ WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDING. HER INTENT WAS NOT GERMANE TO RESOLVING THE ISSUE BEFORE THE HEARING OFFICER, I.E., WHETHER THE CONSTRUCTION WAS IN FACT NEW AND EXEMPT UNDER SECTION 37.2(R)(5) OF THE SAN FRANCISCO ADMINISTRATIVE CODE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR #11918

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Erin C. Shaw, Esq., from Weyand Law Firm, 415-782-6830, for Defendant and moving party
Ethan Schultz, Esq., from Weisbert & Miller, 415-296-7070, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTs PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP OF SAN FRANCISCO, INC. AND DEBORAH LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied as a matter of law. The administrative decision by the Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board did not actually litigate Ms. Lopez's intent. Ms. Lopez was not a party to the administrative proceeding. Her intent was not germane to resolving the issue before the hearing officer, i.e., whether the construction was in fact new and exempt under section 37.2(r)(5) of the San Francisco Administrative Code.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918
